CONFIDENTIAL–Sponsor A Protocol number: VALIDATION_PROTOCOL_001
Amendment 23 (Version 34.0)
Page 1 of 129
TITLE PAGE
Protocol Title: A Phase 3, Randomised, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Multi-Centre Study to 
Evaluate the Treatment Effect of Investigational Product A Compared to Placebo in 
Participants with Knee Osteoarthritis Pain
Protocol Number: VALIDATION_PROTOCOL_001
Amendment Number: 3 (Version 4.0)
Compound: Investigational Product A (PPS)
Brief Title: A study to investigate the treatment effect of subcutaneous injections of pentosan 
polysulfate sodium compared with placebo in adult participants with knee 
osteoarthritis pain.
Study Phase: 3
Sponsor Name: Sponsor A
Legal Registered 
Address: 
8
th Floor, 31 West 34th Street
New York, NY 10001, United States
Local Sponsors: European Union
Sponsor A (Ireland) Limited
Fitzwilliam Hall, Fitzwilliam Place, Dublin 2
Australia
Sponsor A Ltd
Level 15, 500 Collins Street, Melbourne, VIC 3000 Australia
Regulatory Agency/Registry Identifier Number(s):
ClinicalTrials.gov NCT06917404
USA: FDA IND 145770
AUS: TGA CTN CT-2025-CTN-00967-1
MDA: AMDM CTA Rg02-006322
HKG: PPBHK CTC 200926
Version Date: 23 September 202524 February 2026
Confidentiality Statement
Information contained in this protocol is confidential in nature, and may not be used, divulged, published, or otherwise disclosed 
to others except to the extent necessary to obtain approval of the Institutional Review Board or Independent Ethics Committee, or 
as required by law. Persons to whom this information is disclosed should be informed that this information is confidential and 
may not be further disclosed without the express permission of Sponsor A Ltd.

A Phase 3, Randomised, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Multi-Centre Study to Evaluate the Treatment
Effect of Investigational Product A Compared to Placebo in Participants with Knee Osteoarthritis
A study to investigate the treatment effect of subcutaneous injections of Investigational Product A
compared with placebo in adult participants with knee osteoarthritis pain.
The aim of Study VALIDATION_PROTOCOL_001 is to provide evidence of the effect of Investigational Product A
The study will use a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multi-centre design. Efficacy
outcomes for VALIDATION_PROTOCOL_001 will contribute further evidence for PPS as a potential treatment for knee
OA pain. Previous findings from randomised, placebo-controlled studies for knee OA symptoms
performed by the Sponsor (PARA_005 and PARA_OA_008) suggest PPS treatment may have clinically
meaningful outcomes in terms of pain and function.
Results from PARA_005 (n=124) and PARA_OA_008 (n=61) have demonstrated that PPS is efficacious
for pain reduction and improvement of function in participants with OA with moderate to severe knee
pain. Although the maximum PPS treatment effect (pain reduction) generally occurred at Day 56, a
clinically meaningful treatment effect was maintained through to Day 365 for most pain reduction
endpoints, and the change from baseline (CfB) was always higher for the PPS group than placebo.
In PARA_OA_005 (PPS 2 mg/kg twice weekly for 6 weeks) using the Knee Injury and Osteoarthritis
Outcome Score (KOOS) pain scale, a ≥50% reduction in pain was reported in 42% of participants at
Day 53 and in 30% at Day 165 for the PPS group, compared with 31% at Day 53 and 16% at Day 165 for
the placebo group. A similar durable treatment effect on function was observed using the KOOS function
scale. Using the numeric rating scale (NRS) pain scale, a ≥50% reduction in pain was reported in 40% of
participants at Day 53, in 28% at Day 109, and in 15% at Day 165 for the PPS group, compared with
PARA_OA_008 demonstrated improvements in the PPS 2 mg/kg ideal body weight (IBW) twice -weekly
for 6 weeks group, measured by adjusted mean percentage change from baseline (%CfB) using a mixed
model for repeated measures (MMRM). Improvements were seen in Western Ontario and McMaster
and overall (p=0.022) at Day 56. The proportions of participants achieving a ≥30% improvement in the
WOMAC pain subscale score by Day 56 and Day 168 were 73% and 60%, respectively in the PPS
twice-weekly-weekly group, and 55% and 41%, respectively in the placebo group. The proportions of
participants achieving a ≥50% improvement in pain by Day 56 and Day 168 were 60% and 47%,
respectively in the PPS twice-weekly group, and 36% and 32% in the placebo group, respectively.
Durable responses in WOMAC NRS 3.1 Index scores for pain, function, stiffness, and overall were
observed for PPS twice weekly compared to placebo control through to Day 365. Numerical or significant
changes in pain, function (p=0.048), stiffness, and WOMAC overall were demonstrated. The PPS
twice-weekly group maintained a ≥30% improvement in pain (55%) and function (55%) compared to
placebo (33% and 28%, respectively). A ≥50% improvement in function was reported for 55% of
participants in the PPS twice-weeklyweekly group at Day 365 compared to 28% in the placebo group. A
durable response was seen out to Day 365 for pain (p=0.054), function (p=0.048), and overall (p=0.054).
The Patient Global ImpressionsImpression of Change (PGIC) assessment was significantly improved at
Paradigm conducted PARA_OA_002, a 2-stage, adaptive, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled,
multi-centre study to evaluate the dose and treatment effect of PPS compared with placebo in participants
with knee OA pain. To ensure a minimum effective and safe PPS dose regimen, 3 PPS dose regimens,
including a fixed dose regimen, were evaluated during Stage 1 of the study.
In Stage 1 (dose selection), 602 participants were randomised 1:1:1:1 to receive one of 3 doses of PPS or
Given the results from PARA_OA_008 and since the once-weekly dose was included in PARA_OA_002,
but the twice-weekly dose of 2 mg/kg was not, Paradigm requested the Data Monitoring
Committee (DMC) undertake an interim analysis of the performance of all treatment arms in Stage 1 of
The futility analysis revealed that the doses (which did not include 2 mg/kg twice weekly) included in the
dose determination stage of PARA_OA_002 did not perform at the efficacy threshold (effect size
performed when 300 participants had reached Day 56 of follow-up. The findings of the PARA_OA_008
and PARA_005 studies demonstrate that 2 mg/kg twice weekly achieves a clinically important effect size
of this evidence, Paradigm has determined that the 2 mg/kg twice weekly for 6 weeks regimen is the
lowest effective dose to be included in the Phase 3 program.

PGIC-patient global impression of change; PPS–Investigational Product A; WADP–weeklyweekly average of daily pain;
WOMAC–Western Ontario and McMaster Universities (Osteoarthritis Index)
VALIDATION_PROTOCOL_001 is a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multi-centre study that will evaluate
the dose and treatment effect of PPS in participants with knee OA pain.
Eligible patientsparticipants will have a clinical and radiographic diagnosis of knee OA with
by K-L L grade and baseline weekly average of daily pain (WADP) NRS score in the index knee.
The purpose of this study is to measure the change in pain and function with subcutaneous injections of
Investigational Product A (PPS) compared with subcutaneous injections of placebo in participants with
knee OA pain. Study details include:
Approximately 466 participants will be enrolled into this study (Section 9.5).
Participants must be 18 years of age or older, with a clinical diagnosis of OA of the knee, suffering
moderate to severe knee pain, unresponsive to conservative, non-pharmacologic and pharmacologic
therapy, and willing to stop treatment with all other systemic pain medications for the duration of the trial.
The following list only covers KEY inclusion and exclusion criteria. Full reference should be made to
Table 1-2 Key Inclusion and Exclusion Criteria
injections), or participant is unable to take NSAIDs because of contraindication or inability to tolerate.
Table 1-2 Key Inclusion and Exclusion Criteria
endocrinopathies, metabolic joint diseases, lupus erythematosus, joint infections, Paget’s disease, or tumours.
rhino conjunctivitis) or multiple (2 or more) severe allergies.
orequiringRequiring medical regime changes within 60 days before Day 1 due to unstable medical condition.
omentalMental or emotional disorders that preclude reliable study participation.
cardiac bypass surgery or thromboembolic disease.
Participants will be administered twice-weekly subcutaneous (SC) injections for 6 weeks:
o PPS 2 mg/kg twice weekly for 6 weeks or
o placebo twice weekly for 6 weeks.
The maximum duration for each participant is approximately 64 weeks, which includes:
An independent Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) has been appointed for this study.
During the study, the DSMB will review monthly safety reports and perform a formal review of safety
data when 20%, 50%, and 80% of the planned number of participants to be enrolled in the study complete
Additionally, the DSMB will review the results of an interim analysis for futility or early stopping due to
efficacy which will be performed when 50% of participants have been evaluated for the primary endpoint
The DSMB will be informed, within 48 hours of notification to the Sponsor, of any deaths, study stopping
criteria, or adverse events of special interest (AESIs) defined by protocol.
EOS–end of study; IP–investigational product; N–number of participants; PPS–Investigational Product A; SC–subcutaneous.
